title: Expert view: Crude must cool to $70–75 for Nifty 50 to sustain above 25,000, says Shrikant Chouhan of Kotak Securities
summary: Shrikant Chouhan from Kotak Securities emphasizes the critical need for crude oil prices to drop to $70–75 per barrel for the Nifty 50 index to maintain momentum beyond 25,000. He advocates for selective investment in large-cap companies amidst ongoing market uncertainties.

title: ReNew to invest  ₹4,200 crore in 6.5GW solar ingot-wafer unit in Andhra Pradesh
summary: The plant is likely to be commissioned within 24 months and create over 2,100 direct and indirect jobs.

title: Ralph Lauren, Lanvin double down on India luxury market growth amid global slowdown
summary: Ralph Lauren India and Lanvin Group are expanding cautiously but strategically, betting on digital influence and cultural localisation to unlock India’s luxury consumption story.

title: How credit card inactivity affects your credit score and why regular usage supports a strong credit profile
summary: Stop ignoring your credit card; credit score health depends on activity, not inactivity. This article explains how credit card usage habits shape credit score, credit utilisation and long-term financial stability.&nbsp;

title: Quietly moving away from physical gold: ClearTax's Archit Gupta explains how Indians are changing how they invest
summary: This Akshaya Tritiya, gold purchases in India reached  ₹20,000 crore, but physical sales weakened, according to ClearTax's chief executive. Many transactions were made through old gold exchanges, and digital and ETF investments increased.

title: Foreign car companies bet on technology to hang onto once-lucrative China auto market
summary: U.S., Korean and German automakers announced a rush of new, tech-enabled cars at the Beijing Auto Show as they battle a sales slump

title: Hindustan Zinc announces first interim dividend of Rs 11: Check record date for Rs 4,600 crore payout
summary: Metals major Hindustan Zinc on Friday declared a first interim dividend of Rs 11 per equity share for the ongoing financial year 2027, with the record date to determine the eligibility of shareholders being fixed on April 30.

title: Smallcap Shock: 10 stocks crashed up to 45% in CY26 so far. Are you holding any?
summary: Despite relative resilience in the broader smallcap index, several individual stocks have witnessed sharp corrections in CY26, falling up to 45%. The divergence highlights underlying weakness in select counters even as a few outperformers deliver strong gains, pointing to a highly stock-specific market environment.

title: Small-cap stock Krystal Integrated Services jumps 2.5% despite stock market sell-off
summary: Krystal Integrated Services Limited's share price rose 2.5% on April 24 after winning a  ₹138 crore solar contract from Maharashtra. The stock peaked at  ₹643 before settling at  ₹588.70, amidst an overall market decline due to rising crude prices and geopolitical tensions.

title: ‘Would love to own it, but…’: Aswath Damodaran flags $1.75 trillion valuation for SpaceX ahead of IPO
summary: Aswath Damodaran calls SpaceX an engineering marvel with strong competitive advantages, but is wary of the rumoured IPO pricing of $1.75 trillion to $2 trillion.

title: Top Gainers & Losers on April 24: Anant Raj, Infosys, Cyient, Ceat, HCL Tech, Coforge among top losers
summary: The Indian stock market faced a weak session, with key indices declining for the third day due to broad selling in technology stocks and rising crude oil prices. The Nifty dropped 1.10% to 23,907, while the Sensex fell 1.28% to 76,670 amid unfavorable global sentiment.

title: Shriram Finance Q4 Results: Net profit jumps 41% to  ₹3,014 crore YoY, final dividend of  ₹6/share recommended
summary: Shriram Finance Q4 Results: Shriram Finance reported a 41% increase in Q4 standalone net profit to  ₹3,014 crore. NII rose by 21% to  ₹6,751 crore. Profit after tax increased by 20.87% to  ₹9,998.15 crore, and total assets under management grew by 15% to  ₹302,274 crore.

title: KPMG to cut 10% of its audit partners in US to streamline operations as voluntary retirement plan fails
summary: Collectively, layoff announcements by Meta, Microsoft and Nike have led to cutting down of over 24,000 jobs in the US in a single day.

summary: The Bombay High Court on 18 April addressed petitions against pension rejection under the Employees’ Pension Scheme, ruling that employees should not be penalised for employers' lapses. Full details here.&nbsp;

title: Gainers & Losers: Infosys, Adani Energy among 7 big movers on Friday
summary: Indian markets fell sharply with BSE Sensex and Nifty 50 declining amid heavy IT selling. Stocks like Infosys dropped, while select firms including Himadri Speciality Chemical and Adani Energy Solutions gained on earnings and momentum.

title: Sebi clears 4 IPOs including Yatayat Corporation and EAAA India Alternatives
summary: Sebi has approved IPOs for four firms spanning logistics, alternatives, real estate and manufacturing. Companies aim to tap rising demand across sectors, though competitive pressures, cyclicality and evolving investor preferences may shape their growth and market reception.

title: When billionaire Nithin Kamath wanted to earn just Rs 5 crore and retire in Goa
summary: Nithin Kamath warns wealth inequality, driven by asset inflation and concentrated ownership, risks social strain. Reflecting on his journey, he urges rethinking wealth’s purpose, noting idle financial gains benefit few and advocating redirecting capital toward societal impact amid AI-driven change.

title: Sebi eases FPI settlement norms, allows netting of funds in cash market to cut costs, improve efficiency
summary: Sebi has allowed net settlement of funds for foreign portfolio investors in the cash market to reduce costs and improve efficiency. The move enables netting of outright transactions while retaining safeguards for others. It is expected to lower liquidity requirements, streamline operations, and enhance the appeal of Indian markets for global investors.

title: Polymarket gambler makes $35,000 profit by using hair dryer to tamper with temperature readings at Paris airport
summary: A Polymarket user allegedly made $35,000 by exploiting unusual temperature spikes recorded at Paris airport, prompting a police investigation into possible sensor tampering. While speculation points to physical interference, authorities are probing the incident, raising fresh concerns over manipulation risks in prediction-based betting markets.

title: Small-cap stock under  ₹50 Aayush Wellness jumps 9% despite bloodbath on Dalal Street
summary: Aayush Wellness shares rose 9% to  ₹39, hitting a three-month high, with a cumulative gain of 21% over two days. The company launched Liver Care Tablets to address liver health concerns, aiming to capture growth in the nutraceutical market, projected at $18 billion.

title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures surge up to 1.5% amid fresh hopes of US-Iran peace talks; crude falls
summary: Futures for all three indices — S&amp;P 500, Dow Jones and Nasdaq 100 — were trading higher by 0.43, 0.14% and 1.35%, respectively, at the time of writing this report after trading flat earlier.

title: IndusInd Bank Q4 Results: Profit at  ₹594 crore vs loss YoY; NII jumps 43%, asset quality improves
summary: IndusInd Bank reported a net profit of  ₹594.2 crore for Q4 FY26, a significant recovery from last year's loss of  ₹2,329 crore. The improvement was driven by lower provisions and better asset quality, with gross slippages declining sharply.

title: Volatile gold slows Bluestone’s FY27 store expansion pace
